title: Muddy Turkish Lentil Manti
description: A kid-friendly, earthy Turkish lentil manti that uses soft wonton skins and a sweet-tomato lentil filling. A crumbly, comforting dish served with a light yogurt drizzle.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
In a medium pot bring 250ml water to a boil, add the lentils, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan and sauté the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic, cumin, oregano, and tomato paste to the pan and cook for 2 minutes more, stirring constantly.
Drain the cooked lentils and mash them with a fork, then stir in the sautéed onion mixture and season with salt and pepper.
Prepare a shallow dish of water and lay the wonton skins flat on a clean towel, brushing each with a light coat of water to keep them from sticking.
Spoon about a teaspoon of the lentil filling onto each wonton skin, fold the edges over, and press to seal, forming small dumplings.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, drop in the dumplings, and cook for 4-5 minutes until they float and the skins are tender.
Drain the dumplings and let them rest for 2 minutes before transferring to a serving platter.
Whisk the yogurt with lemon juice, drizzle over the dumplings, and sprinkle a pinch of paprika for garnish.
